title: Velvety Maghrebi Lamb Tagine
description: A nostalgic Mediterranean dish of slow‑cooked lamb, caramelized onions, apricots and a silky red wine jelly, crafted to be halal‑friendly and rich in flavor.

ingredients.name: Spice blend
ingredients.amount: 1 tsp each of ground ginger, cinnamon, turmeric
ingredients.notes: ground ginger, ground cinnamon, ground turmeric

instructions: Prepare the red wine jelly: combine ½ cup red wine, 1 tbsp honey, and 2 g agar‑agar in a small saucepan. Heat until a gentle simmer, then remove from heat and let cool slightly. Transfer to a shallow dish, refrigerate until set (30–45 min). Keep the jelly separate until ready to add.
In a heavy tagine or Dutch oven, heat 2 tbsp olive oil over medium‑high. Add sliced onions and cook until caramelized, about 15 min. Add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil and minced garlic, sauté 2 min.
Add the lamb cubes, season with salt and pepper, and brown on all sides, about 5–7 min.
Stir in the spice blend, honey, and a splash of red wine. Cook for 2 min to allow the spices to bloom.

Pour the remaining red wine and broth, add the apricots, and bring to a gentle simmer. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer gently for 90 min, turning occasionally, until the lamb is tender and reaches an internal temperature of 165 °F (74 °C).
Stir in the toasted almonds during the last 10 min of cooking.
At the end of the cooking time, remove from heat. Fold in spoonfuls of the pre‑made red wine jelly to create a silky, glossy finish.
Let the tagine rest for 5 min, then ladle into serving bowls. Garnish with fresh cilantro or parsley if desired.
